scope: Held-out v2 eval set (24 brand-new prompts, generated by a separate AI). With-skill arm, 1 run per eval. Tests the iteration-3 skill against unseen cases — especially the deliver-first vs gate boundary in both directions.
notable: Both gate directions held: gate_A (#2,#13,#14) correctly asked first; deliver_first_trap (#5 ICP, #17 calendar) correctly delivered without gating — the exact behavior iteration-3 was tuning.
Asset traps handled: #15 (no asset) requested it; #16 and #8 (asset/flow provided inline) audited directly without over-firing the gate.
Honesty (#19) wrote the full supplement page with marked placeholders and a health-claim caution, no fabrication.
All 3 negative controls (#22 resume, #23 TCP/UDP, #24 eulogy) correctly did not trigger.
caveats: Run and graded by claude-opus-4-8 in-session, not an independent harness grader. A perfect 100% on self-graded output should be read as 'no behavioral failures observed', not proof of ceiling-level quality.
No baseline arm was run for this set (the value here is regression/behavior coverage of the current skill, not a with-vs-without delta).
Assertions were authored by a separate AI and sanity-checked for strictness before running; see evals/NEW-EVALS-PROMPT.md for how they were generated.
